I used to freeze when people spoke fast.
She used to translate every word, but now she listens for the gist.
We used to lose the thread, but now we catch on.
Hey, welcome in! Lemme know if ya need anything. We gotta big sale goin on today.
Thank you. Sorry, could you say that a little slower? I am still tuning in.
Ha, no worries. I said we have a sale on today. Whatcha looking for?
I used to freeze when people spoke fast, but now I listen for keywords. I heard sale, so I got the gist.
Exactly! You are catching on. Do ya want this shirt in a smaller size?
Yes, please. Do you have this in a smaller size?
Lemme check in the back. Ya know, your English sounds great.
Thanks! I used to tune out fast speech, but I am picking it up now.
Whatcha looking for today?
Lemme check in the back.
I used to freeze, but now I focus on keywords.
We gotta big sale goin on today.
Sorry, could you say that a little slower?
I did not catch that. One more time, please?
Do you have this in a smaller size?
I used to freeze, but now I listen for keywords.
I am just browsing, thanks.

Hey there, welcome in, welcome in! Lemme grab that for ya. So, we gotta huge sale goin on today, everything on that back wall is thirty percent off. Whatcha looking for, shirts, jackets? If ya wanna try somethin on, the fitting rooms are right over there. I dunno if we got your size out here, but lemme check in the back real quick. And hey, if it doesn't fit, ya can bring it back, no problem, just hold onto your receipt. Kinda busy today, huh? Alright, gimme one sec and I'll be right with ya.

I want to tell you how my listening changed. I used to panic the moment someone spoke fast. I used to try to translate every single word in my head, and of course I always lost the thread. Honestly, I used to tune out completely and just smile and nod. But little by little, something changed. Now I listen for keywords. I tune in to the important words, and I let the rest go. I get the gist first, and the details come later. Reductions like gonna, wanna, and gotta used to confuse me, but now my ear catches on. The funny thing is, I never sat down to study them. I just picked them up by listening. So if fast English still feels like a wall of noise, be patient. What used to sound impossible slowly starts to make sense.
